rulings.Case Title: ART WALKER VS. PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014 line 3.DEFENDANTs PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC, PNMAC MORTGAGE OPPORTUNITY FUND INVESTORS, LLC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege an actionable breach of contract; facts asserted in the complaint do not support allegation of breach of paragraph 2 of the Deed of Trust. cause of action for false light is barred by the statute of limitations (See paragraph 33 of Second Amended Complaint). Plaintiff fails to allege tender. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARKET STREET REAL ESTATE PARTNERS LLC VS. NAM INVESTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens And For Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014 line 6. DEFENDANTs NAM INVESTMENTS, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, NANCY MAR's Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens And For Attorneys' Fees And Costs GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to show by preponderance of evidence that probable validity of a real property claim. The only executed contract offered by Plaintiff does not give rise to a real property claim. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN TRIEU et al VS. DARYL J. DUSICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Defendant; Special Interrogatories, Set One' Request For Profuction Fo Documents, Set One; Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014 line 1. PLAINTIFFs KEVIN TRIEU, TRINH LUN's Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Defendant; Special Interrogatories, Set One' Request For Profuction Fo Documents, Set One; Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. Defendant shall personally deliver hard copies of all documents and further responses to Special Interrogatories 2 and 3 to Plaintiff's counsel at 1:30 p.m. on June 18, 2014 (settlement conference). Sanctions GRANTED in the amount of $1,547.50 to be paid no later than June 23, 2014 by 9:00 a.m. by personal delivery to Plaintiff's counsel. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CSV HOSPITALITY MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CARLOS PEELER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014 line 11. DEFENDANT CARLOS PEELER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complains sufficiently alleges cause of action for unlawful detainer per CCP 1161(3). Whether the type of breach asserted in the notice is curable is an issue of fact not properly addressed on a demurrer. Breach alleged in the complaint appears to be of the type that may not be curable. Salton Community Services Dist. v. Southard (1967) 256 Cal.App.2d 526, 529.= (501/REQ)
